Financials Up on Signs of Increased Speculative Activity -- Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose in anticipation of further speculative activity.

Bitcoin prices briefly topped $93,000, in a sign of resurgent risk appetite, easing concerns about another "cryptocurrency winter."

Wall Street money managers are eagerly anticipating a Trump administration initiative to provide young Americans investment accounts.

Toronto-Dominion Bank boosted its dividend payout 2.9% after logging quarterly growth in fee and trading income and domestic-banking volume, which helped counter a hit from restructuring and other charges.

data.title: Dell Technologies Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.525 per Share, Payable Jan. 30, 2026, to Holders of Record Jan. 20
Consumers today want to feel part of a community, Estée Lauder CEO says
data.description:
STORY: :: Stéphane de La Faverie, Estée Lauder Chief Executive Officer 

"I think we are in a world where the funnel - the media funnel - has completely collapsed. So consumers are basically entering brands through very, very different places and TikTok is one of the social media platforms. What I find fascinating today - we're in the world of consumer centricity - and that's why Beauty Reimagined, I say we are really becoming and want to become the best consumer centric beauty company in the world by putting the consumer and recognizing the consumer is in charge today of the communication. Consumers tap into TikTok because TikTok is about communities. They want to feel part of a community. They want to be part of a trance. We're using a lot of TikTok today not only to advertise our brand, but to put our brands in the hands of the creators that through the right style gal, are going to be able to just say, 'This is my interpretation of what Estee Lauder Double Wear Foundation is,' or 'my interpretation of Le Labo Lavande 31 is going to be' and so that gives a lot more creativity and allows us a more personalized communication than ever before, because I believe the world of beauty is about consumer centricity, is about personalization, is about experience and speed, without ever mortgaging quality. And that is the revolution that we are facing - that we want to lead with Beauty Reimagined, and putting out brands, frankly, whether consumers are physically or digitally."

The company, which is in the middle of a turnaround, is also looking to rebalance growth by embracing the Americas and emerging markets, though not at the expense of China, whose consumers have faltered with their spending in recent years, La Faverie added.

Estée Lauder is focused on "local relevance," La Faverie said, adding that the company has taken minority investments in Chinese, Mexican and Indian beauty labels.

data.title: Consumer Cos Down Amid Mixed Economic Signals -- Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies declined amid mixed economic and earnings signals.

Grocery store chain Kroger swung to a fiscal third-quarter loss as expenses increased, though the company said it continues to make progress on its strategic priorities.

Dollar General shares rallied after the deep discounter lifted its outlook for the year. Dollar General's fortunes echoed those of rival Dollar Tree, as both have seen increased customer traffic due to tightening budgets in many American households.

"At least during the last 20 years, when U.S. consumer spending is led by low-income retailers like Walmart, economic growth typically slows," veteran Wall Street strategist Jim Paulsen said in an emailed newsletter. "The recent surge in the relative performance of Walmart stock (the decline in the red line) is a warning signal that U.S. annual real GDP could still weaken further in the coming quarters."

New weekly jobless claims fell by 27,000 to 191,000 in the latest tally.

Hovnanian Enterprises swung to a loss in its fiscal fourth quarter as the homebuilder is handing out more incentives to try to attract wary house-shoppers.

Shares of America's Car-Mart climbed after the used-car dealership posted second-quarter sales above Wall Street's expectations. Rivals CarMax and Auto Nation were flat to lower.

Shares of PVH tumbled after the purveyor of clothing under brands such as Tommy Hilfiger and Calvin Klein posted earnings shy of some investors' expectations.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-04-25 1751ET

data.title: Canada and Mexico should not be export hubs for China, says USTR
Canada to Buy $2.68 Billion in Air-Strike Weapons From Boeing, RTX
data.description:
By Paul Vieira


Canada has agreed to purchase air-strike weaponry from Boeing and RTX as the U.S.'s northern neighbor ramps up military spending.

The U.S. Defense Department said the transaction, with a $2.68 billion price tag, would improve Canada's defense capability and strengthen its ability to contribute to continental security.

One of Prime Minister Mark Carney's initial moves upon winning power this year was to accelerate Canada's defense spending, which failed to meet requirements outlined by the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and triggered widespread criticism from senior U.S. lawmakers and officials.

A representative for Canada's Department of Defence didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

The multibillion-dollar deal comes as the U.S. and Canada remain at an impasse in talks to resolve their tariff row. Carney's office said the prime minister is expected to briefly meet with President Trump on Friday in Washington, on the margins of the World Cup draw at the Kennedy Center.


Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-04-25 1744ET

data.description:
After President Donald Trump put punitive tariffs on products coming in from dozens of countries, there were many "Sell America" calls. But Pimco held onto it stakes in Treasuries and bought more. It turned out to be the right move. Pimco Managing Director, CIO of Core Strategist Mohit Mittal speaks about the strategy on "Bloomberg The Close."

data.title: Health Care Down Sharply on Rotation to Rate-Sensitive Sectors -- Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies fell sharply as traders rotated into sectors more sensitive to changes in interest rates.

Halozyme was granted a preliminary injunction that would prevent Merck & Co. from distributing a formulation of Keytruda in Germany as part of a patent-infringement dispute over the cancer treatment.

The Trump administration's ambition to unleash biomedical innovation and sharpen America's edge against China is colliding with a far messier reality inside the Food and Drug Administration, as Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s revamping of American public health policy has inspired infighting and staff turnover, The Wall Street Journal reported.

Cornell Women's Heart Director on Improving Detection of Aortic Aneurysms
data.description:
According to the Center for Disease Control, the leading cause of death in the United States continues to be heart disease, affecting over 60 million women of all ages. Weill Cornell Medicines Women's Heart Program Director Dr. Nupoor Narula believes its important, especially for women, to participate in research studies when possible to help get a more complete picture of one's personal health. She joined Carol Massar and Alexis Christoferous on 'Bloomberg Businessweek Daily' to examine he underpinnings of heart failure in young women and improving detection of aortic aneurysms.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Energy Up With Oil Futures -- Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies ticked up alongside oil futures amid continued skepticism about the likelihood of peace in Ukraine.

Oil futures in New York closed just shy of the psychologically significant $60-per-barrel level, as doubts about demand continue to offset geopolitical uncertainty.

Escalating military tensions between the U.S. and major oil producer Venezuela are likely to spur crude oil prices, analysts at research firm Rystad Energy said.

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

Ecolab Raises Quarterly Dividend by 12%
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis


Ecolab said its board has approved a 12% rise to its dividend payout, its 34th consecutive annual cash dividend increase.

The new quarterly dividend of 73 cents per share will be payable on Jan. 15, to shareholders of record at the close of business on Dec. 16. The company previously paid a dividend of 65 cents per share.

The new dividend, $2.92 on an annual basis, represents a yield of 1.1%, based on the most recent closing price of $264.42 for the company's stock.

The company last increased its dividend, to 65 cents from 57 cents, in December of last year.

Ecolab said it has paid cash dividends on its common stock for 89 consecutive years.

The company offers water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services.


Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

12-04-25 1716ET
